summary: Blazing Alligator jam & debut Lovelight in a tight two‑song set
blurb: The early‑summer O'Keefe Center show bursts with high energy, featuring a scorching 'Turn On Your Lovelight' and an expansive 'Alligator' jam. Reviewers also note the historic first appearance of TIFTOO and a heartfelt rendition of 'Morning Dew' on alternate recordings.
review: On August 5, 1967 at Toronto’s O’Keefe Center the Dead delivered a brief but unforgettable two‑song set that has become legendary among early‑era collectors. Jerry Garcia tore into the brand‑new ‘Turn On Your Lovelight’ with a powerful tone that set the stage for an almost psychedelic ‘Alligator’ jam, while Bob Weir’s rhythm work kept the groove rock solid. Across the various soundboard sources the performance is praised as “blistering” and “stellar,” and the show also marks the first known outing of the mysterious ‘TIFTOO’ and a moving ‘Morning Dew,’ adding historic weight to an already electrifying night. The recordings are consistently described as clean, well‑balanced soundboards that capture the band’s raw excitement without major drop‑outs, making this early glimpse of the Dead both musically vital and sonically satisfying.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All four recordings are uniformly positive, repeatedly praising the energy of 'Turn On Your Lovelight' and the expansive 'Alligator' jam, noting historic firsts (TIFTOO, Morning Dew) and describing the performance as ‘blistering,’ ‘stellar,’ and ‘high‑energy.’ No negative comments appear, justifying a strong positive sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Excellent early soundboard capture with clear, balanced mix of both songs; regarded as the most complete and clean version.

key_highlights: Jerry’s blistering lead on 'Turn On Your Lovelight' sets a fire‑starter tone.
'Alligator' expands into a psychedelic jam showcasing tight ensemble work.
First known performance of the enigmatic 'TIFTOO'.
A heartfelt, beloved rendition of 'Morning Dew' adds emotional depth.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers a powerful, digging guitar tone that drives both the opening 'Turn On Your Lovelight' and the soaring 'Alligator' jam.
band_performance.Bob: Provides a solid, full‑bodied rhythm foundation, especially evident during the expansive 'Alligator' jam.
